channel.title: Player Choice on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Player Choice on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Restricting Content Based On Player Choice
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about restricting content based on player choice.

item.title: Telegraphing Consequences
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about telegraphing consequences.

item.title: Compromising For The Medium
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about compromising for the medium and basically

item.title: Botched Quests And Player Freedom
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about botched quests and player Freedom.

item.title: NPC Reactions
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about NPC reaction.

item.title: Believable Reactivity
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about believable reactivity and, in other words, how do you make NPCs react to player choices in a way that makes sense in the RPG that you’re making?

item.title: Story Telling And Player Agency
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about storytelling and player agency.

item.title: Beyond Fighting, Sneaking, and Talking
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to brainstorm ideas with you on ways RPGs can move past the fighting, sneaking, talking Triad that, let’s face it, has been done to death since the 90s.

item.title: Personality Roleplay
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about personality roleplay.

item.title: Canonical Endings
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about canonical endings, and by canonical endings I mean the ending of the of a game that is considered to be the right ending, or the correct ending, or the true ending or, at the very minimum, the ending the developer expected you to have.

item.title: Morality Systems
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about morality systems in RPGs.

item.title: Grey Morality
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about gray morality.

item.title: How To Gate Non-Linear Stories
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how to gate nonlinear stories.

item.title: Implementing Endgame Slides
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk to you about implementing endgame slides.

item.title: Player Agency
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim, and let’s start off the new year by talking about player agency.

item.title: Social Conflict Resolution
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about social resolutions in RPGs, and by that I mean combat resolutions that are social, not combat conflict resolutions that are social, it’s hard to talk about.

item.title: Pacifism In Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about pacifism in games.

item.title: Player Hoarding
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about player hoarding.

item.title: Non-Linear Game Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about nonlinear game design.

item.title: The Origin of Reactivity
item.description: Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the origin of reactivity in my computer games.
